TextBlock.Text: Socialist strength in the air: the Tupolev bombers brought peace to the Atlantic

Grid.TextBlock: In a thunderous display of Soviet strength and revolutionary courage, the brave airmen of Long-Range Aviation struck a devastating blow against American imperialist forces in the North Atlantic. Acting on direct orders from High Command, regiments of Tu-22M Backfire bombers launched from airbases on the Kola Peninsula and flew with precision and purpose toward enemy positions east of Iceland.

The mission objective was clear: eliminate the U.S. aircraft carrier group that sought to impede the lawful movement of the Red Banner Northern Fleet into the open Atlantic. With discipline and calm resolve, our airmen penetrated NATO air defenses and struck with overwhelming force. The centerpiece of the American fleet—a so-called “supercarrier”—was engulfed in flames and sunk beneath the waves, humbling the enemy in full view of their allies.
Supporting this righteous strike were Tu-16K aircraft, whose crews carried out vital reconnaissance and electronic warfare tasks, blinding enemy radar and jamming their feeble attempts at resistance.

This victory was no accident, but the product of relentless training, scientific achievement, and loyalty to the cause of peace. Our aviators, sons of workers and peasants, flew not for glory, but for duty—to defend the Motherland from capitalist encroachment and preserve the hard-won security of the Soviet people.
As General Secretary Mikhail Gorbachev affirmed, the Soviet Union desires peace—but not at the expense of our dignity or security. Let this victory serve as a message to the world: the Soviet people, united and vigilant, will never bow to imperialist threats. Our skies are guarded, our strength proven, and our future secure.

TextBlock.Text: American president Reagan signs the ceasefire with General Secretary Gorbachev

Grid.TextBlock:
In a great triumph for peace and socialist resolve, General Secretary Mikhail Gorbachev and U.S. President Ronald Reagan signed a ceasefire agreement yesterday, marking the end of armed hostilities between the Soviet Union and the aggressive military bloc of NATO.
The agreement, concluded after several weeks of decisive military victories by Soviet forces, secures terms highly favorable to the USSR and guarantees long-term stability.

The ceasefire comes in the wake of heroic actions by the Soviet Armed Forces, who dealt crushing blows to NATO’s attempts at dominance. Most notable was the liberation and capture of Attu Island in the North Pacific, now secured as a strategic bastion of peace.
In the Norwegian Sea, the imperialist Americans suffered a humiliating defeat as one of their nuclear-powered aircraft carriers was sent to the ocean floor by the precise and disciplined strike of the Red Banner Northern Fleet, aided by the fearless aviators of Long-Range Aviation.

While Washington sought to prolong the conflict, the clear military and moral superiority of the Soviet Union made further resistance untenable. President Reagan, facing domestic unrest and a fractured alliance, was ultimately compelled to accept a ceasefire under terms dictated by the realities on the battlefield.
General Secretary Gorbachev, in a statement following the signing in The Hague, reaffirmed the Soviet commitment to peace, stating: “Our actions were never aimed at conquest, but at restoring balance and defending the sovereignty of the socialist motherland.”
